topic: A contract that needs three signatures in two countries — how long does that take your business right now?
format: Open with a question that forces the reader to calculate their own answer — time lost, money wasted, or risk carried
post:
A cross-border contract shouldn't take three weeks to close. But for most Oman businesses in 2025, it does.

Not the signing. The chasing.

One signature needed in Oman. Another in the UAE. Your HR manager is on WhatsApp. The other party is on email. Someone printed the wrong version. The finance manager needs to approve the updated terms first.

Three days becomes ten. Ten becomes three weeks.

And somewhere in that gap, a candidate accepted another offer. A vendor moved on. A deal went cold.

This is still how most Oman businesses are running contracts in 2025 — manually, across tools that don't talk to each other, with no paper trail if something goes wrong.

HR software built for Oman shouldn't make that worse. It should make it disappear.

SmartPRO Hub has e-signature contract management built in — bilingual, trackable, no printing, no courier, no chasing anyone on WhatsApp at 9pm. One workflow. Two countries. Done.

Starts at OMR 12/month. 14-day free trial, no credit card needed.

How much time did your last contract lose just to back-and-forth?
